event: The Garden of Earthly Delights (detail) - Paradise Panel
symbols: exotic and fantastical animals — the abundance and wonder of unfallen creation
central fountain or organic structure — the Fountain of Life in Paradise or the Tree of Life
pink sphere with dark opening — the mysterious origin of creation, the generative force of the Creator
green rolling hills — the Edenic landscape before the Fall
unicorn — purity and innocence in the primordial garden
description: A detail from Bosch's Garden of Earthly Delights showing the left Paradise panel. A verdant green landscape teems with exotic and fantastical animals. A white elephant, a giraffe, a unicorn, and numerous birds populate the rolling green hills. At center, a tall pink organic fountain-like structure rises, branching into coral-like forms. At lower center, a large pink sphere with a dark circular opening sits in a pool of water. Bizarre rock formations and surreal organic structures dot the background in blue and green.
theological_description: This detail from the Paradise panel of Bosch's triptych depicts the Garden of Eden in its primordial splendor, teeming with the creatures of the fifth and sixth days of creation (GEN.1.20-25). The exotic animals — elephant, giraffe, unicorn — suggest the fullness of creation's diversity before the Fall corrupted the natural order. The central fountain or Tree of Life recalls Genesis 2:9-10, where the tree of life stood in the midst of the garden and a river flowed out to water it. The surreal, fantastical quality of Bosch's vision suggests the incomprehensible beauty of the world as God intended it — a beauty that transcends human imagination because we only know creation in its fallen state. The abundance of creatures peacefully coexisting fulfills the Edenic peace that Isaiah prophesied would return in the messianic age: 'The wolf shall dwell with the lamb' (ISA.11.6).

scripture_refs.reason: God created the living creatures of sea, air, and land — the teeming animals of this paradise
The Lord God planted a garden in Eden with the tree of life and a river
The peaceable kingdom where animals coexist in harmony — the Edenic peace depicted
God saw everything that he had made, and behold, it was very good
The river of life and the tree of life in the new Jerusalem — paradise restored
